summary: Stellar Estimated>Shakedown sequence, tight jams, huge drum mix
blurb: The show shines with an explosive Estimated Prophet → Shakedown Street stretch and a rare standalone Fire on the Mountain. Jerry's guitar is alive and cooking while the drums roar crystal‑clear. Highlights include Jack Straw, Sugaree and a soaring Friend of the Devil.
review: November 24, 1978 at the Capitol Theatre captured one of the Dead’s late‑’70s high points. The first set launches with a punchy Jack Straw and Sugaree before cruising through Me & My Uncle into a tight Big River and Stagger Lee. The second set is where the magic really clicks – an “Estimated Prophet → Shakedown Street” sequence that reviewers called “legendary,” followed by a rare, un‑segmented Fire on the Mountain and a soaring Friend of the Devil that rides a jazzy pocket. Jerry’s guitar was described as “alive and cooking” despite his raspy, sick voice, and the drum sound was repeatedly praised as huge, clear, and perfectly mixed – even guest percussionist Hamza al‑Din earned kudos during the Drums. Keith’s keyboards sit front‑and‑center on I Need a Miracle and Shakedown Street, rescuing the mix when it gets muddy, while Bob delivers an explosive solo on The Promised Land. Overall, the performance is tight, energetic, and full of those unexpected curveballs that keep a Dead show unforgettable.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Seven recordings are clearly positive (praising the Estimated>Shakedown stretch, crystal‑clear drums, and vibrant guitar work) while only two note negatives (Jerry's strained vocals). The consensus is strong enthusiasm across multiple sources, justifying a high positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystalline highs, deep bass and a vivid mix that showcases the band’s tight performance and huge drum sound.

key_highlights: Estimated Prophet → Shakedown Street sequence is a show‑stopper
Rare standalone Fire on the Mountain adds historic value
Jack Straw and Sugaree open the set with high energy
Crystal‑clear, massive drum mix highlighted across recordings

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar was "alive and cooking" with perfect tone; despite a raspy, sick voice his leads and solos remained stellar.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers an explosive second solo on The Promised Land, adding extra fire to the set.
band_performance.Keith: Front‑and‑center keyboards, especially on I Need A Miracle and Shakedown Street, rescuing the mix when it gets muddy.
band_performance.Mickey: Keeps a solid pocket throughout; praised alongside Bill for tight drumming, with guest Hamza al‑Din adding extra drive during the Drums segment.
band_performance.Billy: Provides steady, punchy rhythms that complement Mickey’s textures and drive the high‑energy moments.
